title: celtis pallida var. pallida
intro: Celtis pallida var. pallida, also known as the Desert Hackberry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The Desert Hackberry has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. The plant's health benefits include its ability to support the immune system, reduce inflammation, and act as an antioxidant, making it a popular choice for herbal remedies. Celtis pallida var. pallida can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, allowing for various herbal preparations to harness its medicinal properties.
